unicode.value: SdA Propuesta para el alumnado
SdA Propuesta para el alumnado
master class
closed exercise or problem
real project
didactic game
open problem
conceptual map
Sección
SdA Propuesta para el alumnado
Propuesta para el alumnado
Text and Tasks
Be safe, be smart!
Text and Tasks
Situación de Aprendizaje centrada en el uso seguro de internet y redes sociales, desarrollando la competencia comunicativa en inglés en contextos reales
Text and Tasks
PORTADA - Be safe, be smart!
Contextualización

Nivel: 2.1

Módulo: Sociedad y cultura en lengua extranjera

Temporalización: 6 sesiones

Producto final: Infografía + Podcast
Text and Tasks

• Comprender riesgos
  • Usar vocabulario
  • Usar verbos modales
  • Crear productos
Text and Tasks

• Comunicación lingüística
  • Digital
  • Personal y social
Text and Tasks
Entorno digital de aprendizaje

Entorno digital de aprendizaje:

Las actividades se desarrollarán y/o presentarán a través del Aula Virtual del centro, utilizando herramientas digitales para la realización de tareas, entrega de productos y acceso a materiales, además de para favorecer la comunicación entre docente y alumnado.

Se emplearán recursos como:
• Plataforma educativa (Aula Virtual)
• Herramientas de creación digital (Canva, Genially)
• Recursos multimedia (vídeos, podcasts)
Text and Tasks
Sesión 1 - Activación

• Introducir el tema: Redes sociales y riesgos
  • Activar conocimientos previos
  • Comprender ideas globales de un texto
Text and Tasks

Debate: Do you use social media?
Work in pairs or small groups
Text and Tasks
Vídeo - Staying safe online

Watch this video and answer the questions:

Questions:

  • What risks appear in the video?
  • What should you NOT do online?
  • What must you do to stay safe?
  • Have you ever received a scam message?

Metodología:

  • Active Learning
  • Communicative Approach
  • Audiovisual Comprehension (CE1)
  • Real-life situations
Text and Tasks
Lluvia de ideas

Brainstorming: What risks can you find on social media?

Examples: - Fake accounts

                   - Scams

                   - Sharing personal information
Text and Tasks

Reading: A Story About Online Risks

Laura is a young woman who uses social media every day. She enjoys sharing photos, chatting with friends, and following her favorite influencers.

One day, she receives a message from a stranger. The message looks friendly and includes a link. The person says it is something interesting and asks her to click on it.

Laura is curious, so she clicks on the link. Suddenly, something strange happens. She cannot access her account anymore. Her password does not work.

Later, she discovers that her account has been hacked. The stranger was a scammer.

Now Laura understands an important lesson: she must be careful online. She should not click on unknown links, and she must protect her personal information.

Comprehension Questions:

  1. Who is Laura?
  2. What message does she receive?
  3. What happens when she clicks the link?
  4. What lesson does she learn?
Text and Tasks
Actividad interactiva. Safe or Unsafe. Pregunta de Elección Múltiple
Enter the available choices here. You can add options by clicking the "Add Another Option" button. Delete options by clicking the red "X" next to the Option.
University of Auckland

Type in the feedback that you want the student to see when selecting the particular option. If you don't complete this box, eXe will automatically provide default feedback as follows: "Correct answer" as indicated by the selection for the correct answer; or "Wrong answer" for the other options.
Enter a hint here. If you do not want to provide a hint, leave this field blank.
Select the correct option by clicking on the radio button.

Although more often used in formal testing situations MCQs can be used as a testing tool to stimulate thought and discussion on topics students may feel a little reticent in responding to. When designing a MCQ test consider the following:
  • What learning outcomes are the questions testing
  • What intellectual skills are being tested
  • What are the language skills of the audience
  • Gender and cultural issues
  • Avoid grammar language and question structures that might provide clues
Enter the question stem. The quest should be clear and unambiguous. Avoid negative premises as these can tend to be ambiguous.

When building an MCQ consider the following:
  • Use phrases that learners are familiar with and have encountered in their study
  • Keep responses concise
  • There should be some consistency between the stem and the responses
  • Provide enough options to challenge learners to think about their response
  • Try to make sure that correct responses are not more detailed than the distractors
  • Distractors should be incorrect but plausible
Enter the question stem. The quest should be clear and unambiguous. Avoid negative premises as these can tend to be ambiguous.
You use the same password for all your accounts
Enter a hint here. If you do not want to provide a hint, leave this field blank.

Evaluación

• Direct Observation
  • Participation in activities
  • Global understanding of the text
Text and Tasks
Metodologías Activas

• Aprendizaje basado en tareas
  • Gamificación
Text and Tasks
Sesión 2 - Léxico y uso funcional
Session 2 - Vocabulary for online safety
Text and Tasks
Objetivos didácticos

Objetivos de la sesión:

  • Comprender y utilizar vocabulario básico relacionado con la seguridad digital
  • Identificar situaciones seguras y de riesgo en el entorno online
  • Utilizar el léxico en intercambios orales sencillos
  • Participar en tareas cooperativas de construcción de significado
Text and Tasks
Competencias específicas + Criterios

Competencias específicas 

  • CE1: Comprender el sentido general e información específica de textos orales y escritos breves y sencillos
  • CE2: Producir textos orales y escritos breves, sencillos y comprensibles


Criterios de evaluación asociados:

  • Identificar vocabulario básico en contextos cotidianos (CE1)
  • Utilizar léxico aprendido en producciones guiadas (CE2)
Text and Tasks
Vocabulario (Representación - DUA)

Key Vocabulary (Online safety)

- password → secret word to access your account
- privacy → control of your personal information
- account → personal online profile
- scam → attempt to steal information or money
- link → clickable web address
- stranger → unknown person

DUA - Representación. Lenguaje claro y definición simple

Apoyo a la comprensión (CE1)
Text and Tasks
Actividad 1 - Matching (CE1)

Activity 1: Match the word with the definition

password → ______
privacy → ______
scam → ______
a) A trick to steal information
b) Secret word
c) Personal information control

Criterio: Identificación de léxico en contexto (CE1)

Metodología: Aprendizaje basado en tareas
Text and Tasks
Actividad 2 - (Interactiva)
Text and Tasks
Enter the available choices here. You can add options by clicking the "Add Another Option" button. Delete options by clicking the red "X" next to the Option.
University of Auckland

Type in the feedback that you want the student to see when selecting the particular option. If you don't complete this box, eXe will automatically provide default feedback as follows: "Correct answer" as indicated by the selection for the correct answer; or "Wrong answer" for the other options.
Enter a hint here. If you do not want to provide a hint, leave this field blank.
Select the correct option by clicking on the radio button.

Although more often used in formal testing situations MCQs can be used as a testing tool to stimulate thought and discussion on topics students may feel a little reticent in responding to. When designing a MCQ test consider the following:
  • What learning outcomes are the questions testing
  • What intellectual skills are being tested
  • What are the language skills of the audience
  • Gender and cultural issues
  • Avoid grammar language and question structures that might provide clues
Enter the question stem. The quest should be clear and unambiguous. Avoid negative premises as these can tend to be ambiguous.

When building an MCQ consider the following:
  • Use phrases that learners are familiar with and have encountered in their study
  • Keep responses concise
  • There should be some consistency between the stem and the responses
  • Provide enough options to challenge learners to think about their response
  • Try to make sure that correct responses are not more detailed than the distractors
  • Distractors should be incorrect but plausible
Enter the question stem. The quest should be clear and unambiguous. Avoid negative premises as these can tend to be ambiguous.
1. You receive a message with a suspicious _______

A suspicious link can be dangerous.

A _______ is a person you don't know.

A stranger is someone you don't know.

Enter the question stem. The quest should be clear and unambiguous. Avoid negative premises as these can tend to be ambiguous.
You must protect your _____

Your password must be private.

A ______ is a trick to steal your information.

Type in the feedback that you want the student to see when selecting the particular option. If you don't complete this box, eXe will automatically provide default feedback as follows: "Correct answer" as indicated by the selection for the correct answer; or "Wrong answer" for the other options.
Text and Tasks
Actividad 3 - Fill in the gaps (CE2)

Complete the sentences:


1. This message is a ________

2. Protect your ________

3. Do not click on a suspicious ________

Criterio: Uso guiado del vocabulario (CE2)

Metodología: Aprendizaje basado en tareas
Text and Tasks
Actividad 4 - Mini-Glosario

Group activity.

In small groups, create a Mini-Glossary with:

  • word
  • definition
  • example sentence

Example: 

  • pasword - set of characters that you use to access your account - You must protect your password

Metodología: Aprendizaje cooperativo

                         Aprendizaje basado en tareas

Competencia: CE2
Text and Tasks
Actividad 5 - Expresión oral (Interacción CE3)

Ask and answer. Use short answers.

• What is a scam?

• What is a password?

• Is it safe to click unknown links? 

Competencia: CE3 (Interactuar en situaciones comunicativas sencillas)

Metodología: Enfoque comunicativo

                        Aprendizaje activo
Text and Tasks
Evaluación

Evaluación de la sesión:

Criterios:

• Comprende vocabulario básico en contexto (CE1)

• Utiliza léxico en actividades guiadas (CE2)

• Participa en interacción oral sencilla (CE3)

Instrumentos:

• Observación directa

• Actividades interactivas

• Producción oral
Text and Tasks

Atención a la diversidad (DUA):

• Representación: vocabulario con definiciones claras

• Acción y expresión: actividades orales, escritas e interactivas

• Implicación: trabajo cooperativo y actividades gamificadas
Text and Tasks
Metodologías Activas

• Task-Based Learning: used in matching, gap-fill and classification activities
  • Cooperative Learning: used in the mini-glossary group task
  • Communicative Approach: used in speaking activities
  • Active Learning: present in decision-making tasks (safe/unsafe)
Text and Tasks
Sesión 3 - Verbos modales

• Comprender el uso de los modales can, could, must, have to, should
  • Expresar obligación, posibilidad y consejo
  • Utilizar estructuras modales en situaciones cotidianas
  • Participar en intercambios orales sencillos dando consejos
Text and Tasks
Competencias + Criterios

Competencias específicas:

CE1: Comprender estructuras básicas en textos sencillos
CE2: Producir mensajes breves con corrección básica
CE3: Interactuar en situaciones comunicativas sencillas


Criterios de evaluación:

  • Identifica el uso de modales en contexto (CE1)
  • Utiliza modales en frases guiadas (CE2)
  • Participa en intercambios orales dando consejos (CE3)
Text and Tasks
Contenido gramatical

Modal Verbs for Online Safety:

must → obligation
👉 You must protect your password
have to → obligation (external rule)
👉 You have to check your privacy settings
can → possibility / ability
👉 You can block unknown users
should → suggestion
👉 You should change your password


💡 Use these verbs to give advice and talk about safety online
Text and Tasks
Actividad 1 - Identificar

1. Identify the modal verb:

  1. You must protect your password → ______
  2. You can block users → ______
  3. You have to check settings → ______

Objetivo: reconocer estructuras (CE1)
Text and Tasks
Actividad 2 - Transformación

2. Rewrite the sentences using modal verbs.

1. Protect your password → You ______ protect your password

2. Block unknown users → You ______ block unknown users

3. Check your privacy → You ______ check your privacy settings


Competencia: CE2 (producción guiada)
Text and Tasks
Actividad 3 - Uso guiado

3. Complete the sentences.

You ______ protect your personal information
You ______ click on suspicious links
You ______ talk to strangers online


👉 Possible answers: must / can / shouldn’t

Competencia: Uso funcional del lenguaje (CE2)
Text and Tasks
Actividad 4 - Role-play (Speaking activity)

Speaking Activity: Role-play

Student A: You have a problem online
Student B: Give advice

Example:
A: I received a message from a stranger
B: You must not answer. You should block the user

Change roles

Competencia: CE3 (interacción)
Metodología: Enfoque comunicativo
                         Aprendizaje activo
Text and Tasks
Evaluación

Evaluación de la sesión:

Criterios:

  • Reconoce el uso de modales (CE1)
  • Utiliza estructuras modales en frases (CE2)
  • Participa en interacción oral (CE3)    

Instrumentos:

  • Observación directa
  • Actividades escritas
  • Role-play
Text and Tasks

Atención a la diversidad (DUA):

  • Representación: ejemplos claros y estructurados
  • Acción y expresión: actividades escritas y orales
  • Implicación: role-play y situaciones reales
Text and Tasks
Metodologías activas

• Task-Based Learning (used in Activities 1,2,3)
  • Communicative approach + Active Learning (used in Role-play)
Text and Tasks
Sesión 4 - Aplicación y gamificación

Objetivos de la sesión:

  • Consolidar el vocabulario y los modales trabajados
  • Aplicar el idioma en situaciones reales
  • Desarrollar la toma de decisiones en contextos digitales
  • Participar activamente en actividades gamificadas
Text and Tasks
Competencias + Criterios

Competencias específicas:

CE1: Comprender información en textos sencillos
CE2: Producir mensajes breves con intención comunicativa
CE3: Interactuar en situaciones comunicativas cotidianas


Criterios de evaluación:

  • Identifica riesgos en situaciones digitales (CE1)
  • Utiliza vocabulario y modales correctamente (CE2)
  • Participa activamente en actividades grupales (CE3)
Text and Tasks
Actividad 1. Quiz interactivo. Pregunta de Elección Múltiple
Enter the available choices here. You can add options by clicking the "Add Another Option" button. Delete options by clicking the red "X" next to the Option.
University of Auckland

Type in the feedback that you want the student to see when selecting the particular option. If you don't complete this box, eXe will automatically provide default feedback as follows: "Correct answer" as indicated by the selection for the correct answer; or "Wrong answer" for the other options.
Enter a hint here. If you do not want to provide a hint, leave this field blank.
Select the correct option by clicking on the radio button.

Although more often used in formal testing situations MCQs can be used as a testing tool to stimulate thought and discussion on topics students may feel a little reticent in responding to. When designing a MCQ test consider the following:
  • What learning outcomes are the questions testing
  • What intellectual skills are being tested
  • What are the language skills of the audience
  • Gender and cultural issues
  • Avoid grammar language and question structures that might provide clues
Enter the question stem. The quest should be clear and unambiguous. Avoid negative premises as these can tend to be ambiguous.

When building an MCQ consider the following:
  • Use phrases that learners are familiar with and have encountered in their study
  • Keep responses concise
  • There should be some consistency between the stem and the responses
  • Provide enough options to challenge learners to think about their response
  • Try to make sure that correct responses are not more detailed than the distractors
  • Distractors should be incorrect but plausible
Enter the question stem. The quest should be clear and unambiguous. Avoid negative premises as these can tend to be ambiguous.
What should you do if you receive a message from a stranger?
Enter a hint here. If you do not want to provide a hint, leave this field blank.

You can block users to stay safe.

Is it safe to talk to strangers online?
Enter a hint here. If you do not want to provide a hint, leave this field blank.
Enter the available choices here. You can add options by clicking the "Add Another Option" button. Delete options by clicking the red "X" next to the Option.
No, it is not safe

Type in the feedback that you want the student to see when selecting the particular option. If you don't complete this box, eXe will automatically provide default feedback as follows: "Correct answer" as indicated by the selection for the correct answer; or "Wrong answer" for the other options.

You can report or block fake accounts.
Actividad 2 - Breakout educativo

Breakout Activity: Solve the Challenge

Work in groups. Solve the following situations to “escape”:

1. A fake account sends you a message
👉 What do you do?
2. You receive a link with a prize
👉 Is it safe or unsafe? Why?
3. Someone asks for your password
👉 What should you say?


🎯 Use modal verbs (must, can, should)

Metodología:

  • Gamificación
  • Aprendizaje cooperativo
  • Aprendizaje basado en problemas
Text and Tasks
Actividad 3 - Casos reales

Real-Life Situations

Read and decide:

Case 1:
You receive a message: “You won a prize! Click here.”

Case 2:
A stranger wants to follow you and asks personal questions

Case 3:
Your friend uses the same password for everything

👉 Answer:

  • Is it safe or unsafe?
  • What should you do?

Metodología:

  • Aprendizaje basado en tareas
  • Aprendizaje activo
  • Pensamiento crítico
Text and Tasks
Evaluación

Evaluación de la sesión:

Criterios:

  • Reconoce situaciones de riesgo (CE1)
  • Aplica vocabulario y modales correctamente (CE2)
  • Participa en la resolución de actividades (CE3)

Instrumentos:

  • Observación directa
  • Resultados del quiz
  • Participación en actividades grupales
Text and Tasks

Atención a la diversidad (DUA):

  • Representación: situaciones reales y ejemplos claros
  • Acción y expresión: respuestas orales y escritas
  • Implicación: actividades gamificadas y trabajo en grupo
Text and Tasks
Metodologías activas

• Task-Based Learning + Gamificación (used in Quiz)
  • Gamificación + Cooperative Learning + Project-Based learning (used in Breakout)
  • Task-Based Learning (Real-life situations)
Text and Tasks
Sesión 5 - Producto final

Objetivos de la sesión:

  • Crear productos comunicativos en inglés
  • Integrar vocabulario y modales en contextos reales
  • Desarrollar la competencia digital
  • Trabajar de forma cooperativa
Text and Tasks
Competencias + Criterios

Competencias específicas:

CE2: Producir textos orales y escritos breves
CE3: Interactuar en tareas comunicativas


Criterios de evaluación:

  • Produce mensajes claros usando vocabulario y modales (CE2)
  • Participa en la creación de productos cooperativos (CE3)
Text and Tasks
Presentación del proyecto

Final Task: Stay Safe Online

You will create two products:

  • An interactive infographic about safety rules for social media including clear images and examples and using modal verbs.
  • A podcast with safety advice including a ten-point guide (Decalogue) to good practices on social media in English using modal verbs and related specific vocabulary.


Work in groups and use English

Metodología: Aprendizaje basado en proyectos
Text and Tasks
Actividad 1 - Decálogo

Step 1: Create a Safety Decalogue

In groups, write 10 rules:

Examples:

  • You must protect your password
  • You should not click unknown links
  • You can block users

Metodología: 

  • Task-Based Learning
  • Cooperative Learning
Text and Tasks
Infografía

Product 1: Interactive Infographic

Create an infographic with:

- Safety rules
- Images and icons
- Clear structure
- Use digital tools (Canva, Genially, etc.)

You must include: 

- Vocabulary related to the topic

- Modal verbs

Products must be created and uploaded in the Virtual Classroom.

Metodología: 

  • Project-Based Learning
  • Design Thinking
Text and Tasks
Actividad 3 - Podcast

Product 2: Podcast

Record a short podcast:

- Present your safety rules
- Speak clearly
- Use modal verbs
Example:
“You must protect your password”

Metodología: 

  • Project-Based Learning
  • Communicative Approach
Text and Tasks
Evaluación

Evaluación:

Criterios:

  • Utiliza el vocabulario correctamente (CE2)
  • Utiliza los verbos modales correctamente (CE2)
  • Comunica ideas de forma clara (CE3)


Instrumentos:

  • Productos finales
  • Observación directa
  • Producción oral
Text and Tasks

Atención a la diversidad (DUA):

  • Representación: modelos visuales y orales
  • Acción y expresión: infografía y podcast
  • Implicación: trabajo creativo y colaborativo
Text and Tasks
Metodologías activas

• Task-Based Learning + Cooperative Learning (used in Decalogue)
  • Design Thinking + Project-Based learning (used in Infographic)
  • Project-Based Learning + Communicative Approach (Podcast)
Text and Tasks
Sesión 6 - Presentación y Evaluación final

Objetivos de la sesión:

  • Presentar los productos finales en inglés
  • Escuchar y valorar los podcasts
  • Reflexionar sobre el proceso de aprendizaje
  • Desarrollar la autoevaluación y coevaluación
Text and Tasks
Competencias + Criterios

Competencias específicas:

CE2: Producir textos orales y escritos breves
CE3: Interactuar en situaciones comunicativas


Criterios de evaluación:

  • Presenta información de forma clara (CE2)
  • Participa en la exposición oral (CE3)
  • Evalúa el trabajo propio y de los compañeros
Text and Tasks
Presentación de productos

Final Presentation

Students will:

- Present their infographic
- Explain their safety rules in English
- Share their group work with classmates through the Virtual Classroom Platform

Metodología: 

  • Service-Learning
  • Real communication
Text and Tasks
Escucha del Podcast

Podcast Listening

Listen to all group podcasts and think about:

- Are the safety rules clear?
- Is the vocabulary correct?
- Do they use modal verbs properly?
Text and Tasks
Exposición oral

Oral Presentation

Each group:

- Presents their work
- Explains their rules
- Answers simple questions
Example:

“What is your most important rule?”
“Why is it important?”
Text and Tasks
Autoevaluación y Coevaluación

Self and Peer Assessment

Students reflect on:

- What did I learn?
- What did I do well?
- What can I improve?

Peer feedback:

  • I liked your presentation because…
  • You could improve…
Text and Tasks
Evaluación final

Evaluación final:

Criterios:

  • Comunicación oral clara (CE2)
  • Participación en las presentaciones (CE3)
  • Reflexión sobre el proceso de aprendizaje

Instrumentos:

  • Rúbrica final
  • Observación
  • Autoevaluación del alumnado
Text and Tasks

Attention to diversity (DUA):

  • Representation: oral presentations and visual products
  • Expression: speaking, listening and reflection
  • Engagement: participation and collaborative evaluation
Text and Tasks
Metodologías activas

• Service-Learning + Cooperative Learning (Presentation)
  • Active learning (Podcast listening)               
  • Self and Peer Assessment (Metacognition + Reflective Learning)
  • Communicative Approach (Oral presentation)
Text and Tasks
